title: brain jobs run — --json success output
description: Claim and drain queued jobs synchronously under the exclusive jobs-runner process lock (D5). A second concurrent runner exits 2 `locked:jobs-runner`. Selectors are the SSOT `[<jobId> | --all]` (mutually exclusive; --all is the default when neither is given). Bulk selection is deterministic (ordered by (next_run_at, jobId)); each job runs independently and exactly once. Output is the canonical batch object `{ items, aggregate }` — never the single-error envelope. A job that changes state mid-batch is `skipped:state-changed`, not an error.

errorEnvelope.description: The complete CLI error envelope (error-envelope.schema.json) — used per-item for a failed job and at the top level for a whole-batch failure. Kept as a local $def (not a cross-file $ref) so each schema compiles standalone under the harness's per-file Ajv.

items.description: One entry per processed job, in deterministic (next_run_at, jobId) order. Each item is a discriminated result-OR-error: a result carries an outcome and never an error; a failed item carries outcome=failed plus the standard error envelope.

oneOf.description: A non-failed result. No `error` may accompany it.

runId.description: Present for action-required (e.g. a Tier-3 review-pending run) and any run-producing outcome.

retryAfterMs.description: Provider-directed retry timing propagated from a rate_limit/quota error.

oneOf.description: A per-item failure: outcome=failed with the standard error envelope required.

error.description: Optional whole-batch failure envelope (e.g. the drain aborted before per-item processing). Present only for a batch-level failure; per-item failures live in items[].

aggregate.description: Batch roll-up. exitCode is chosen by the deterministic precedence 4 ⊐ 2 ⊐ 1 ⊐ 7 ⊐ 6 ⊐ 5; skipped/cancelled outcomes do not raise it above 0 on their own.

x-atlas-contract.summary: Claim + drain queued jobs synchronously (single-runner lock).

args.name: <jobId>
args.description: Drain a single job; mutually exclusive with --all.

flags.name: --all
flags.description: Drain every eligible job (default when neither <jobId> nor --all is given).

flags.name: --idempotency-key <key>
flags.description: Caller idempotency key for the drain invocation.

sideEffects: acquires the exclusive jobs-runner lock for the drain
advances job state + attempts + backoff per jobs-contract.md
each executed job's workflow writes its own ledger/audit records
prohibitedEffects: never runs concurrently with another jobs-runner (exclusive lock)
no job executes more than once

locks.note: acquired per draining job that integrates

errorCodes.when: <jobId> and --all both given, or unknown flag/argument
another jobs-runner holds the exclusive lock
ledger-writing is blocked pending a verified backup
--idempotency-key reused with a different request (same key, changed selector)
a concurrent duplicate with the same --idempotency-key is still in progress (never re-drains)
